title: Chapter 47 – Encounter
content:
The next morning, mission preparations were complete. Jeong Yeon-shin received a travel pack containing various supplies, provisions, and travel expenses.

As a blue warrior, they said he bore full responsibility.

Additionally, the characters engraved on the Desolate Sect martial uniform had been covered with silk panels. The craftsmanship was quite exquisite.

"This is a mission dealing with famous families. Since it's dangerous, we don't bother with full disguises."

Do Yu-won from the General Administration Office nodded at Ma Jin's words.

"Official Desolate Sect martial uniforms contain thread from a celestial being called the Heaven Silkworm. It's quite valuable — there's one thread at each vital point, embroidered vertically. It can buy extremely brief moments against horizontal slashes or thrusts."

"Since reinforcing with iron plates would be spotted by any discerning master. It would draw unnecessary attention."

"How could you cover this?! The very emblem of Sir Jeong and me!"

Hyeon Won-chang feigned outrage.

While stroking the 'Hwang' character on his own right shoulder with his left hand, he caressed the one below Jeong Yeon-shin's shoulder with his right.

It was deeply unsettling. Jeong Yeon-shin edged away.

Hyeon Won-chang's face crumpled slightly.

"Isn't Mount Hua Sect enough? I find it hard to accept. Now Desolate Sect will soon be going too!"

"Quiet down, Desolate Divine Hero."

At Ma Jin's calm words, Baek Mi-ryeo stepped forward. Only then did Hyeon Won-chang shrink back.

Retrieving to a safe distance, he muttered things no one could parse. Junior Shin So-bin shook her head.

"Aren't you the same age as Senior Lightning Flash? I have to wonder if the secret journey will go smoothly."

"Sir Hyeon is different from how he appears. I'm sure your worry is needless."

"I hope so. It's my first mission too, and we'll need to coordinate from here on. I only caught a glimpse of Senior Lightning Flash's martial arts during the promotion ceremony. I'm curious whether you can match your reputation while traveling the martial world."

Shin So-bin lifted her lips. It was a bold smile.

Jeong Yeon-shin wasn't particularly interested. He simply hoped she wouldn't be a liability.

Reaching blue level wasn't the end — the next promotion uniform, black level, carried the rank of squad leader.

The gap between blue and black was even vaster than from white to blue.

'I've taken one step. From here, there can be no mission failures.'

The anxiety he had felt upon failing to subdue the Blood Flame Sect's Seventh Apostle resurfaced.

He never wanted to experience that again. That was why it didn't matter whether the Hwangbo family's young master possessed the Solar Divine Meridian or anything else.

Accumulating merit was everything. Jeong Yeon-shin clasped his fists to Ma Jin.

"See you later."

"Yes. The moment you complete the mission, we clash with the Hwangbo family. Let's meet then."

Ma Jin said with a nod. After a brief silence, when he spoke again, the scar at his mouth corner carried something sheepish.

"...Missions can fail. Think of your safety first."

He knew those words didn't come from the Radiant Demon Wing Leader. Jeong Yeon-shin bowed silently to his uncle.

Unlike maternal grandfather Ma Yeon-jeok, he couldn't simply push away Ma Jin, who carried genuine guilt and kept trying to bridge the distance.

Jeong Yeon-shin shifted his gaze slightly.

"Don't overdo it later either, squad leader. They say the Hwangbo family head is one of the foremost masters in Shandong."

"It's not a sparring match. It's war. We'll attack together."

Ma Jin replied with a faint smile.

Shaking his head as if disappointed, Jeong Yeon-shin mounted the brown fine horse beside him.

Feeling the saddle settle firmly beneath him, he patted the horse's neck.

Back at the Jeong family house, he had thought of horses as lifelong companions, like the legendary thousand-li steeds. Things were different here.

Desolate Sect rotated its horses constantly, making it hard to grow attached.

"Please take care of me until the post station."

Jeong Yeon-shin leaned forward and whispered to the horse. Then he raised the reins as the gathered Radiant Demon Wing seniors saw them off.

Hyeon Won-chang and Shin So-bin flanked him on horseback.

"Back into the martial world. Always thrilling."

Hyeon Won-chang said with a grin. Jeong Yeon-shin caught Shin So-bin casting a skeptical sideways glance before he flicked the reins.

It was his first mission as a Desolate Sect blue warrior.

* * *

Just covering the Desolate Sect mark felt like stepping into a different world.

Was it the weight of leading the group without seniors? He felt an emptiness despite Hyeon Won-chang's presence.

The background that had made people show respect — or fear — had vanished.

'I've heard there's also contempt that Desolate Sect masters face in the murim.'

Jeong Yeon-shin thought. Since he hadn't experienced it himself, whatever came would feel different from the stories.

Their destination was Huizhou in South Zhili. A post station route ran in a slight arc from Xiangyang to Huizhou.

Unlike previous missions where they'd needed long rest intervals for the horses, the group galloped hard.

Shin So-bin rode expertly, befitting a direct descendant of a prestigious family.

Though long-distance riding was another matter entirely, she proved no hindrance.

Hyeon Won-chang also rode well. He claimed that touring the martial world should be savored after missions.

Twenty days passed. Jeong Yeon-shin didn't yet possess the internal force to traverse between cities using lightness technique.

The two white-level warriors were no different. They had to rely on horses.

Nothing of note happened until they neared Huizhou.

This held true despite forest bandits and river bandits lurking everywhere.

When Hyeon Won-chang had traveled from Shaanxi to Xiangyang for the Desolate Exam, he claimed to have encountered countless ones — enough that his stories sounded like pure boasting.

'I'm getting a feel for it.'

Meanwhile, Jeong Yeon-shin had been refining his sword songs. He was working to gradually advance into the realm of sound techniques.

He was also dismantling the chants of Moon Spirit Harmony Technique. It seemed possible to create new internal energy techniques before the Dragon Phoenix Gathering concluded.

Woong.

They had reached the stretch of official road approaching Huizhou.

Beneath an unusually large ginkgo tree beside the road, Northern Light — resting across Jeong Yeon-shin's crossed legs — hummed.

The Desolate Sword wasn't with him. Since he had to conceal his affiliation, he had left it with Cheong Myeong.

'Amplifying the sword's resonance has become second nature.'

Infusing internal energy into a sword to prevent it from breaking easily. A common technique among masters.

Jeong Yeon-shin went further.

He felt the essence of sound. Using Fate Defying Scripture's principle, he split two strands of true energy and fed them into the blade.

The sound grew louder as the finely rippling energy waves intensified.

Goong!

The wonder of true energy never ceased to amaze.

Extremely delicate manipulation — difficult even with innate talent — produced an unprecedented resonance.

He could control the pitch.

Sound techniques were born from imbuing radiating true energy into the melody that spread outward from this vibration.

'The power of this martial art will ultimately depend on internal energy quantity.'

But there were further complications. Sound techniques were themselves a profound martial discipline.

Jeong Yeon-shin's sword songs, with their shallow years of development, couldn't distinguish friend from foe. He thought of Hyeon Won-chang and Shin So-bin.

Send a sound transmission warning them to shield their ears with internal force before unleashing the technique? How convenient it would be if fights always allowed that much preparation.

Even so, the sense of achievement was considerable. In one-against-many battles, the effectiveness would be devastating.

"You said sword songs? It certainly commands attention."

It was Shin So-bin, dressed in white. Her dark pupils sparkled with interest.

She approached with rustling steps, placed her hands on her knees, and bent at the waist.

Her long, tied hair spilled forward in a glossy cascade.

Jeong Yeon-shin couldn't fathom why someone who wasn't even a master would grow their hair that long.

It was practically an invitation to be grabbed during close combat.

"Sorry."

Jeong Yeon-shin apologized, letting his stray thoughts drift past.

Huizhou was an enormous city.

Merchant groups ferrying high-grade tea leaves or silver coins reportedly came and went constantly through this thriving hub.

The relatively safe official road went without saying. Plenty of passersby on the opposite lane stole glances their way.

"It's quite pleasant to listen to! There's real elegance in it!"

Hyeon Won-chang grinned as he tore into provisions.

Was it because the 'Hwang' characters were concealed? He seemed oddly delighted by the attention.

That was when a group slowly approached.

"Looking at them, they seem bound for the Dragon Phoenix Gathering. Truly arrogant."

"Making sword sounds in broad daylight without even fighting? What is that, if not showing off mediocre martial arts?"

They were young men and women radiating formidable energy.

They wore luxurious yellow silk martial uniforms, but Jeong Yeon-shin's eyes went first to the swords at their waists.

Martial artists. They looked like scions of prestigious families.

A youth wearing a hero's headband across his forehead — much like Hyeon Won-chang — and a woman with slightly upturned eyes.

Both appeared around twenty. Their faces still carried traces of youth.

Behind them trailed groups that looked like attendants.

Before Hyeon Won-chang could say anything, Jeong Yeon-shin rose to his feet.

"I was inconsiderate. Your words strike true."

When he offered a respectful clasped-fist greeting, the man and woman's postures shifted. They returned the gesture identically, as though etiquette had been drilled into them since birth.

The response seemed almost reflexive, yet their bearing had discipline.

"...He admits fault readily. Not bad."

"Maybe because we let our energy waves show in irritation. He must have been intimidated."

Both were arrogant yet composed. It immediately brought to mind the Dragon Phoenix Gathering.

They said many rising talents from famous families would attend. These two surely counted among them.

"Your name and sect?"

The man asked, chin slightly raised.

They said there were as many varieties among famous families under heaven as there were stars. Etiquette was no exception.

While there were humble ones like Sword Dragon Wei Ji Myo-hwa, tales of arrogant prodigies from orthodox sects were hardly rare.

'They seem like martial family people.'

Jeong Yeon-shin thought. These could be the martial nobles who reigned like kings over their settled territories.

This was an era when gatherings like the Dragon Phoenix were infrequent.

Having lived like royalty in their own little worlds — that was precisely why incidents like the Hwangbo family's forced labor could happen.

The youth raised his eyebrows.

"What are you pondering? Speak plainly."

"I am Gu Gwa of the Chasing Truth Sect."

"Chasing Truth Sect? Never heard of it. Someone without roots, then."

The woman beside the youth declared without mercy. Faint contempt settled across both their faces. Their tone shifted accordingly.

The youth spoke again, thick eyebrows twitching.

"Right. As you can see, we are bloodline of an orthodox famous family. You've heard of the Hwangbo family? Since I know every orthodox sect attending the Dragon Phoenix Gathering, if your sect falls between righteous and evil, I can't let you go quietly."

"I must educate you. You, who can carelessly produce the kind of sword resonance that belongs to a higher realm."

The woman stepped forward, adding her piece. She seemed thoroughly accustomed to satisfying her whims by wielding prestige.

"......"

The Hwangbo family? Though Huizhou lay just ahead, for a moment Jeong Yeon-shin couldn't believe it.

The description Ma Jin had provided of the young master's appearance came to mind belatedly.

The face bore a resemblance.

He had heard Ma Jin's story, told over drinks during the banquet celebrating his blue-level promotion.

His sister — Jeong Yeon-shin's mother — had been married into the Jeong family in a manner that was almost absurd.

They said martial world fate struck like sudden wind and rain. Was this situation any different?

He felt the energy waves of Hyeon Won-chang and Shin So-bin strengthening behind him.

Though he had assumed it would take time, it could end quickly too. He offered silent thanks to Buddha and the Primordial Heavenly Lord.

There was no need for restraint.

Since his task was to handle the Hwangbo family's young master and provoke him into mobilizing the family's power, bloodshed in the orthodox realm had always been part of the mission.

'We should definitely secure proper justification.'

First, he lowered his clasped hands and parted his lips.

"You speak of roots."

Jeong Yeon-shin let his true nature show without restraint.

"Though the Hwangbo family's prestige is undeniable, your attitude after hearing my sect's name is unconscionable. Is that murim courtesy? Your insult has stained my sect's honor. Each side should address the other's shortcomings. How can a martial artist speak like this?"

"What...?"

Was it something they had never encountered? For a moment, all expression vanished from the man's and woman's faces.

Jeong Yeon-shin sensed onlookers beginning to gather.

He continued in a clear voice.

"I offered courtesy first and apologized. I gave my sect and name even in the face of your unreasonable words and behavior. You speak of lacking roots while flaunting the Hwangbo name? Could you endure it, in my place? I request a sparring match."

"Words flowing like a mountain stream."

The corners of the youth's mouth curled upward. He raised his arm and pushed back the woman, whose fine brows were drawn tight.

Blood vessels protruded on the forearm revealed between his sleeves. He had clearly refined extraordinary blood energy.

"Good. I never liked that a sect between righteous and evil was invited to the Dragon Phoenix Gathering. My title is—"

"Not interested. Come."

Cutting him short, Jeong Yeon-shin slowly gripped Northern Light's hilt.

Suddenly, Sword Art Flower of the Deep Martial Alliance — whose head he had taken — flashed through his mind.
